title: Thou hidden love of God, whose height
firstLine: Thou hidden love of God, whose height,
tuneAttribution: Tune: Henri Frederick Hemy (1818–1888), James George Walton (1821–1905).
publicDomainBasis: Gerhardt Tersteegen (1697–1769), Henri Frederick Hemy (1818–1888), James George Walton (1821–1905). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Thou hidden love of God, whose height,
Whose depth unfathomed no man knows,
I see from far Thy beauteous light,
Inly I sigh for Thy repose;
My heart is pained, nor can it be
At rest, till it finds rest in Thee.

sections.label: Verse 2
Verse 3
sections.text:
’Tis mercy all, that Thou hast brought
My mind to seek her peace in Thee;
Yet, while I seek but find Thee not,
No peace my wand’ring soul shall see.
Oh, when shall all my wand’rings end,
And all my steps to Thee-ward tend!

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Is there a thing beneath the sun
That strives with Thee my heart to share?
Ah! tear it thence, and reign alone,
The Lord of every motion there.
Then shall my heart from earth be free,
When it has found repose in Thee.

sections.label: Verse 5
sections.text:
Oh, hide this self from me, that I
No more, but Christ in me may live;
My vile affections mortify,
Nor let one darling sin survive.
In all things nothing may I see,
Nothing desire or seek but Thee.

sections.label: Verse 6
sections.text:
O Lord, Thy sovereign aid impart,
To save me from low-thoughted care
Chase this self-will through all my heart
Through all its latent mazes there
Make me Thy duteous child, that I
Ceaseless may Abba, Father, cry.

sections.label: Verse 7
sections.text:
Each moment draw from earth away
My heart which lowly waits Thy call;
Speak to my inmost soul and say
“I am Thy Love, Thy God, Thy All.”
To feel Thy power, to hear Thy voice,
To taste Thy love, be all my choice.
